text: The Earth orbits around the sun, not the other way around, though it took humanity thousands of years to understand this properly. For most of recorded history, people believed the sun and stars revolved around a stationary Earth because that's what direct observation appears to show from our perspective on the planet's surface. It wasn't until Copernicus, Galileo, and Newton developed better models and telescopes that we realized our apparent observations were misleading. This shift in understanding required letting go of an intuitive but incorrect assumption, which is one of the hardest things humans have to do when pursuing truth.
